goal: Land Phase 3 of the visual redesign — additive --mt-* token namespace, global chrome re-skin (header, bottom-nav, footer), card-back visual on the daily and question-draw selection screens, and a new flip animation timing with reveal glow.
done: Appended a Phase-3 :root block to css/theme-tokens.css with the --mt-* token namespace (plum/rose/gold/shadow/glow/radius/nav/streak — additive only, no override of existing --color-* tokens). Created css/phase-3-redesign.css containing §1 global chrome rules (site-header, bottom-nav, site-footer) and §2 card-back override scoped narrowly to body[data-page='daily'] .card .back and body[data-page='question-draw'] .card .back. Edited the flip animation block in css/styles.css to use var(--mt-flip-duration, 650ms) with var(--mt-flip-easing, cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)) and added @keyframes mt-reveal-glow that runs once on .card.revealed .back (animation, not transition — transition can't fade from one static value to another on a single class toggle). Wired phase-3-redesign.css into all 12 EN+TH pages via a stylesheet link after phase-2-1-logo.css. cp canonical → www/ and npx cap sync ios completed; md5 canonical==www verified for the three CSS files plus index.html and th/index.html; phase-3 link confirmed present in ios mirror.
affects: all 12 EN+TH pages — site-header, bottom-nav, site-footer re-skinned with --mt-* tokens
daily.html and th/daily.html plus the question-draw selection board — card-back now deep plum-to-rose with gold inner border
card-flip timing across all reading flows — duration up to 650ms with cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) easing, one-shot reveal glow on .card.revealed
notes: User's spec named the flip-state class as .card.flipped; the JS in js/reading.js actually toggles .card.revealed, so the new animation rule targets .card.revealed .back. User's spec referenced share/share.js for the poster canvas; the file is share/poster.js. Poster canvas palette (dozens of hex values for the old dark-navy palette — #0f1429, #f8d77a, #f7f4ee, #aab0c9, etc) was NOT touched this round — flagged to user for a follow-up phase. Per-screen redesigns (per-page backgrounds, result/profile/full-reading layout work) deliberately deferred to later phases. desktop-unchanged is NOT claimed: site-header rule applies globally now so desktop pastel chrome is affected.
verified: md5 canonical == www for css/theme-tokens.css, css/phase-3-redesign.css, css/styles.css, index.html, th/index.html
grep phase-3-redesign.css === 1 in all 12 canonical pages and the corresponding ios mirror pages
EN/TH css link-set parity diff: index.html vs th/index.html identical phase sequence
cap sync ios completed in 106ms, no plugin errors
working tree was reset with git checkout -- www/ ios/ before edits to discard pre-existing mirror drift

goal: Phase 3.1 follow-up: swap the bright/navy accent palette in share/poster.js canvas code to match Phase-3's --mt-* mappings, while preserving the dark navy base and other semantic colors.
done: Applied four strict color-swap rules to share/poster.js via a Python regex pass: (1) '#f8d77a' → '#c9933a' (3 swaps — bright gold to plum-gold), (2) rgba(236,205,132,x) → rgba(201,147,58,x) preserving opacity (8 swaps — gold rgba family), (3) '#2A3556' → '#3d1a5c' (1 swap — upright tone primary), (4) rgba(R,G,B,x) where R∈[17,22], G∈[18,24], B∈[26,48] → rgba(61,26,92,x) preserving opacity (4 swaps — dark navy translucent pills/overlays to plum-translucent). Total 16 replacements. Deliberately untouched per spec: poster background '#0f1429', cream text tones (#f7f4ee, #fff7de, #fff9eb), lucky-color swatch palette (lines 1580-1593), pure white rgba surfaces, all rgba(0,0,0,x) shadows.
affects: all share-poster canvas renders (Daily, Question, Full) — gold accents shift from bright-yellow to plum-gold, dark-navy translucent pills/overlays shift to plum-translucent, upright tone primary text on light panels shifts from navy to plum
notes: Strict bounds for Rule 4 left two outlier rgba dark-navy values untouched: rgba(11,13,26,0.55) at line 1959 (a drop shadow) and rgba(15,18,38,0.62) at line 2063 (a panel fill). Both are visually adjacent to the family that was swapped but fall outside the user-specified bounds; flagged here in case a future pass wants to extend the rule. Visual QA not performed — manual browser test required to confirm contrast on dark-bg panels with new plum tones still reads.
verified: md5 canonical == www == ios mirror after cp + cap sync
grep new tokens '#c9933a|#3d1a5c|201,\s*147,\s*58|61,\s*26,\s*92' share/poster.js: 15 matched lines (16 swaps; one line had two same-family rgbas)

goal: Phase 3.2 fixup for the Phase 3 card-back rule. QA surfaced a selector miss: the original body[data-page='daily'/'question-draw'] .card .back selector did not match any rendered markup on the selection board.
done: Replaced the §2 block in css/phase-3-redesign.css. Old rule targeted .card .back (nested), but the actual rendered DOM on daily.html and question.html selection boards is per js/main.js lines 360-369. Two issues with the old rule: (a) selector mismatch — no .card>.back nesting exists on these pages, (b) even retargeted, an with src set is opaque and covers any CSS background on itself. New rule sets the gradient on the parent .card-slot so it is visible only as a graceful FALLBACK when the PNG fails to load (CDN miss / offline). The real card-back identity remains an asset-side decision filed for a follow-up. cp + cap sync ios completed; md5 canonical == www == ios.
affects: daily.html, th/daily.html, question.html (question-draw board state), th/question.html — .card-slot now paints the plum-to-rose gradient as fallback; visible only when the card-back PNG fails to load. When the PNG loads normally (the common case) the visual is unchanged from before Phase 3.2.
notes: Phase 3 commit (da14d5b) deliberately not amended — three commits exist between it and HEAD (1cecd97 log, f9ea423 phase 3.1, 1fab430 phase 3.1 log), so a new Phase 3.2 commit pair is cleaner than rewriting recent history. node --check js/main.js run as precaution even though no JS file changed — passed. Visual QA still pending in browser: open daily.html, force a card-back PNG 404 (e.g. block /cards/* in DevTools), confirm slot shows plum-to-rose gradient.
verified: md5 canonical == www == ios for css/phase-3-redesign.css after cp + cap sync
grep 'card-slot' css/phase-3-redesign.css: 3 matches (2 selector lines + 1 comment); grep 'card .back' returns no live rules (only the comment)
node --check js/main.js: OK (no JS change in this commit but verified as precaution)
filed: Card-back asset redesign — generate plum-to-rose gradient card-back PNGs in the Phase-3 redesign palette for all 11 decks (Velvet Familiar, Honey Hour, Moonveil, Linden Bloom, Boba Oracle, Meow Nakorn, Overtime Oracle, Pawbit, Sugar Paws, Sushicat, InkMess) and upload to R2 via wrangler r2 object put --remote. Until then, the Phase 3 plum-to-rose visual on selection-board card backs only renders as a CDN-failure fallback.

goal: Phase 3.3 fixup for the Phase 3 chrome rules. Local browser QA surfaced that the Phase 3 selectors lost the cascade on the homepage — phase-1-home.css §2 rules (body[data-page='home'] .site-header, etc) had higher specificity (0,2,1) than phase-3-redesign's bare .site-header (0,1,0), AND phase-1-home.css loaded after phase-3-redesign on index.html / th/index.html. Result: nothing in Phase 3 chrome painted on the homepage; the user reported 'the browser shows the old design'.
done: Two changes. (1) Rewrote phase-3-redesign.css chrome selectors with a body[data-page] attribute-prefix (matches every page that has a data-page attribute — all of them) raising specificity to (0,2,1) to tie phase-1-home and phase-2-1-logo. Six chrome selectors updated: .site-header, body.has-bottom-nav .site-header (in media query, now body[data-page].has-bottom-nav .site-header), .bottom-nav, .bottom-nav__item, .bottom-nav__item.is-active, .site-footer. §2 .card-slot rules already had body[data-page='daily'/'question-draw'] specificity and were left untouched. (2) Swapped the link order on index.html and th/index.html so phase-3-redesign.css loads LAST — after phase-1-home.css — so source order resolves ties in phase-3's favor. Non-homepage pages already had phase-3 as the last css link and required no change. cp + cap sync ios; md5 canonical == www == ios.
affects: all 12 EN+TH pages — phase-3 chrome (header surface + box-shadow, bottom-nav surface, footer surface) now actually wins the cascade and paints. Homepage was the breaking case — it now renders the --mt-nav-bg and --mt-shadow-panel tokens instead of phase-1-home's older values.
notes: Diagnostic chain that found the bug: user reported 'old design'; ran 5 read-only checks (file exists, links present, tokens defined, link order, var refs) — all PASS — then realized the cascade specificity was the actual blocker by comparing phase-1-home.css's body[data-page='home'] (0,2,1) selectors to phase-3-redesign's bare-class (0,1,0) selectors. Saved as a feedback memory candidate: always check specificity of competing rules when 'file is present but invisible'. Visual QA against meowtarot.com after push, not local file://.
verified: grep '<link.*phase-' on all 12 pages — phase-3-redesign.css is the last css link on every page
grep 'body\[data-page\]' css/phase-3-redesign.css — 6 selectors prefixed (header, has-bottom-nav header, bottom-nav, bottom-nav__item, bottom-nav__item.is-active, site-footer)
md5 canonical == www == ios for css/phase-3-redesign.css, index.html, th/index.html
cap sync ios completed; no plugin errors
